title: TCP listener — socket accept loop via completion IO
purpose: TCP listeners are the entry point for network servers. In BEAM, gen_tcp:listen returns a listening socket port. In beamr's completion model, the listener is an FdResource backed by a socket FD. Accepting connections uses io_uring's multishot accept (IORING_ACCEPT_MULTISHOT) which submits a single SQE and receives a CQE for each accepted connection — far more efficient than resubmitting after each accept. Each accepted connection is wrapped as a new FdResource with the connected client's address.
task: Implement TCP listen and accept BIFs. Listen creates a bound, listening socket as an FdResource. Accept submits an Accept operation to the ring and suspends the process until a connection arrives. Support both single-shot and multishot accept patterns.

requirements.title: tcp_listen BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:tcp_listen/2 is called with a port number and options, THE SYSTEM SHALL create a listening TCP socket, bind it to the specified port, call listen(), and return an FdResource term.
acceptance: erlang:tcp_listen/2 registered as BIF with args (Port :: non_neg_integer(), Options :: list())
Binds to 0.0.0.0:Port (or specified address from options)
Calls listen() with backlog from options (default 128)
Returns FdResource wrapping the listening socket FD
Test: listen on a random port, verify FdResource returned

requirements.title: tcp_accept BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:tcp_accept/1 is called with a listening FdResource, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit an Accept operation to the CompletionRing, suspend the calling process, and on completion return {ok, ConnFd} where ConnFd is a new FdResource for the accepted connection.
acceptance: erlang:tcp_accept/1 and erlang:tcp_accept/2 (with timeout) registered as BIFs
Submits Accept { listener_fd } to the ring
On completion: create new FdResource for the accepted FD, return {ok, ConnFd}
Timeout variant: if no connection within timeout ms, return {error, timeout}
Test: listen, connect from another thread, accept, verify FdResource for connection

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement data transfer (send/recv) — that is B-107
SHALL NOT implement active/passive mode — that is B-108
SHALL NOT implement multishot accept as the default — single-shot is simpler and sufficient for initial implementation
SHALL NOT implement IPv6 — IPv4 first, IPv6 as a follow-on
verification: cargo test passes with listen + accept cycle
Accepted connection FdResource has correct peer address
Listener close after accept in progress returns error to waiting process
